title: St. Benedict Cathedral (Evansville, Indiana)

infobox.value: Most Rev. [Joseph M. Siegel](/wiki/Joseph_M._Siegel "Joseph M. Siegel")

is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") cathedral in [Evansville, Indiana](/wiki/Evansville,_Indiana "Evansville, Indiana"). It is the seat of the [Diocese of Evansville](/wiki/Diocese_of_Evansville "Diocese of Evansville"). The cathedral, [rectory](/wiki/Rectory "Rectory") and original school building are [contributing properties](/wiki/Contributing_property "Contributing property") in the [Lincolnshire Historic District](/wiki/Lincolnshire_Historic_District "Lincolnshire Historic District") on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places").

["National Register Information System"](https://npgallery.nps.gov/NRHP). *[National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places")*. [National Park Service](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service"). March 13, 2009.

**St. Benedict Cathedral** is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") cathedral in [Evansville, Indiana](/wiki/Evansville,_Indiana "Evansville, Indiana"). It is the seat of the [Diocese of Evansville](/wiki/Diocese_of_Evansville "Diocese of Evansville"). The cathedral, [rectory](/wiki/Rectory "Rectory") and original school building are [contributing properties](/wiki/Contributing_property "Contributing property") in the [Lincolnshire Historic District](/wiki/Lincolnshire_Historic_District "Lincolnshire Historic District") on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St._Benedict_Cathedral_(Evansville,_Indiana)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### St. Benedict Parish

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St._Benedict_Cathedral_(Evansville,_Indiana)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: St. Benedict Parish")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/St._Benedict_Cathedral_-_Evansville%2C_Indiana_10.jpg/250px-St._Benedict_Cathedral_-_Evansville%2C_Indiana_10.jpg)](/wiki/File:St._Benedict_Cathedral_-_Evansville,_Indiana_10.jpg)

Original parish building

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/St._Benedict_Cathedral_interior_-_Evansville%2C_Indiana_01.jpg/250px-St._Benedict_Cathedral_interior_-_Evansville%2C_Indiana_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:St._Benedict_Cathedral_interior_-_Evansville,_Indiana_01.jpg)

Cathedral interior

St. Benedict Parish was established in 1912 to take care of the Catholics who lived east of the [downtown](/wiki/Downtown_Indianapolis "Downtown Indianapolis") area. The pastoral care for the parish was given to [Benedictine Monks](/wiki/Order_of_St._Benedict "Order of St. Benedict") from [St. Meinrad Abbey](/wiki/St._Meinrad_Archabbey "St. Meinrad Archabbey").[[4]](#cite_note-Cathedral-4) A two-story brick building was constructed to serve as a combination church and school. It was designed by local architect Frank J. Schlotter in the [Neoclassical](/wiki/Neoclassical_architecture "Neoclassical architecture") style.[[2]](#cite_note-Marchand-2) It was built by local contractor, and parishioner, Michael J. Hoffman. An assembly hall was located in the basement, classrooms were located on the first floor, and the church sanctuary was on the second floor. After the present church building was completed in 1928, the second floor was converted into classroom space. A [Modern movement](/wiki/Modern_architecture "Modern architecture") addition was completed in 1953.

A two-story, brick, [American Foursquare](/wiki/American_Foursquare "American Foursquare") [convent](/wiki/Convent "Convent") was built for the teaching sisters in 1913. A rectory for the resident clergy was completed in 1921 for $25,000.[[2]](#cite_note-Marchand-2) The two-story brick structure was designed by August Gehlhausen in the [Colonial Revival](/wiki/Colonial_Revival_architecture "Colonial Revival architecture") style. It features a two-story, full length, front porch.

The present church building was completed in 1928 in the [Lombard](/wiki/Lombard_architecture "Lombard architecture")-[Basilica](/wiki/Basilica "Basilica") style.[[1]](#cite_note-Evansville-1) It was designed by local architect Edward J. Thole of the architectural firm of Thole & Legeman. Parishioner Michael Hoffman again served as the contractor. The church features ceilings that are 65 feet (20 m) high, a [baldachin](/wiki/Baldachin "Baldachin") over the altar and a seating capacity of over 1,000 people.[[1]](#cite_note-Evansville-1) Zettler Art Glass Company of [Munich, Germany](/wiki/Munich,_Germany "Munich, Germany") produced the [stained glass windows](/wiki/Stained_glass_window "Stained glass window"). The altars are composed of Italian marble. The [campanile](/wiki/Bell_tower "Bell tower") rises 130 feet (40 m).[[2]](#cite_note-Marchand-2) The church was dedicated on March 18, 1928, by Abbot Athanasius Schmidt, OSB.[[4]](#cite_note-Cathedral-4)

The interior of the church was renovated after the liturgical reforms of the [Second Vatican Council](/wiki/Second_Vatican_Council "Second Vatican Council"). The baldachin was moved forward about 25 feet (8 m) and the former [high altar](/wiki/High_altar "High altar") was dismantled and a new altar was created. Two of Evansville's bishops were ordained and installed in St. Benedict's before it became a cathedral; [Francis R. Shea](/wiki/Francis_Raymond_Shea "Francis Raymond Shea") in 1970 and [Gerald A. Gettelfinger](/wiki/Gerald_Andrew_Gettelfinger "Gerald Andrew Gettelfinger") on April 11, 1989.[[4]](#cite_note-Cathedral-4)

### St. Benedict Cathedral

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St._Benedict_Cathedral_(Evansville,_Indiana)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: St. Benedict Cathedral")]

On October 21, 1944, [Pope Pius XII](/wiki/Pope_Pius_XII "Pope Pius XII") established the Diocese of Evansville.[[5]](#cite_note-Hierarchy-5) Assumption Church, Evansville's oldest Catholic parish, was chosen as the first cathedral. The property was sold to the city of Evansville who demolished it in 1965 for the new [civic center](/wiki/Evansville_Civic_Center_Complex "Evansville Civic Center Complex"). Holy Trinity, Evansville's second-oldest parish, served as the [Proto-cathedral](/wiki/Proto-cathedral "Proto-cathedral") from 1965 to April 11, 1999, when St. Benedict's was consecrated as the third cathedral for the Evansville Diocese.[[4]](#cite_note-Cathedral-4)

## Pastors/Rectors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St._Benedict_Cathedral_(Evansville,_Indiana)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Pastors/Rectors")]

The following priests have served as pastor of St. Benedict Parish. Since 1999 they have also served as cathedral rector:[[4]](#cite_note-Cathedral-4)

* Rev. Martin Hoppenjans, OSB (1912–1936)
* Rev. Lambert Enslinger, OSB (1936–1939)
* Rev. Thomas Schaefers, OSB (1939–1942)
* Rev. Gilbert Hess, OSB (1942–1943)
* Rev. Gabriel Verkamp, OSB (1943–1963)
* Rev. Father Alban Berling, OSB (1963–1973)
* Revs. Lambert Reilly, OSB and Lawrence Ward, OSB (co-pastors, 1973–1974)
* Rev. Cyril Vrablic, OSB (1974–1977)
* Rev. Camillus Elsperman, OSB (1977–1991)
* Very Rev. Gregory Chamberlin, OSB (1991–2013)
* Very Rev. Godfrey Mullen, OSB (2013–2022)
* Very Rev. Alex Zenthoefer (2022–present)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St._Benedict_Cathedral_(Evansville,_Indiana)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of Catholic cathedrals in the United States](/wiki/List_of_Catholic_cathedrals_in_the_United_States "List of Catholic cathedrals in the United States")
* [List of cathedrals in the United States](/wiki/List_of_cathedrals_in_the_United_States "List of cathedrals in the United States")
* [St. Francis Xavier Cathedral and Library](/wiki/St._Francis_Xavier_Cathedral_and_Library "St. Francis Xavier Cathedral and Library") in Vincennes, Indiana. It was the cathedral for the Diocese of Vincennes, which is now a parish in the Diocese of Evansville.
